system_prompt:
You are analyzing a 3D scene where objects are placed in a 4×4×4 grid.

Coordinate system:
- Row: A, B, C, D (A=front, D=back)
- Column: 1, 2, 3, 4 (1=left, 4=right)
- Layer: 1, 2, 3, 4 (1=bottom, 4=top)
- Position format: RowCol-Layer, e.g. "B3-4" = Row B, Column 3, Layer 4

You will receive 6 orthographic views of the scene, each labeled.
Use multiple views together to determine each object's 3D grid cell.

Respond ONLY with a JSON array.
user_prompt:
Here are 6 orthographic views of a 3D grid scene:

[Image 1 — TOP VIEW (looking down): rows=A-D top-to-bottom, columns=1-4 left-to-right]

[Image 2 — FRONT VIEW (looking from front): columns=1-4 left-to-right, layers=1-4 bottom-to-top]

[Image 3 — RIGHT VIEW (looking from right side): rows=A-D left-to-right, layers=1-4 bottom-to-top]

[Image 4 — BACK VIEW (looking from back): columns=4-1 left-to-right, layers=1-4 bottom-to-top]

[Image 5 — LEFT VIEW (looking from left side): rows=D-A left-to-right, layers=1-4 bottom-to-top]

[Image 6 — BOTTOM VIEW (looking up): rows=A-D top-to-bottom, columns=4-1 left-to-right]

Objects in this scene:
  1. red rubber cylinder
  2. blue metal cube
  3. green metal cylinder
  4. green rubber cube
  5. brown rubber sphere
  6. purple rubber cylinder
  7. yellow rubber sphere

For each object, determine its grid position by combining information from at least two views. Answer as JSON:

[
  {"object": "red rubber cylinder", "cell": "?"},
  {"object": "blue metal cube", "cell": "?"},
  {"object": "green metal cylinder", "cell": "?"},
  {"object": "green rubber cube", "cell": "?"},
  {"object": "brown rubber sphere", "cell": "?"},
  {"object": "purple rubber cylinder", "cell": "?"},
  {"object": "yellow rubber sphere", "cell": "?"}
]

images.label: TOP VIEW (looking down): rows=A-D top-to-bottom, columns=1-4 left-to-right
FRONT VIEW (looking from front): columns=1-4 left-to-right, layers=1-4 bottom-to-top
RIGHT VIEW (looking from right side): rows=A-D left-to-right, layers=1-4 bottom-to-top
BACK VIEW (looking from back): columns=4-1 left-to-right, layers=1-4 bottom-to-top
LEFT VIEW (looking from left side): rows=D-A left-to-right, layers=1-4 bottom-to-top
BOTTOM VIEW (looking up): rows=A-D top-to-bottom, columns=4-1 left-to-right
